title: Selective Octahedral Accommodation of Cr$^{3+}$ and Weak Magnetic Connectivity in the Sugilite Analogue KNa$_2$Cr$_2$Li$_3$Si$_{12}$O$_{30}$
authors: Yuya Haraguchi, Taishu Aoki, Daisuke Nishio-Hamane, Hiroko Aruga Katori
abstract: We report the synthesis of the Cr analogue of sugilite, KNa$_2$Cr$_2$Li$_3$Si$_{12}$O$_{30}$, in the milarite-type framework. Rietveld refinement of a composition-conserving antisite model gives $x = 0.0024(18)$ in KNa$_2$[Cr$_{2-x}$Li$_x$][Li$_{3-x}$Cr$_x$]Si$_{12}$O$_{30}$, corresponding to a T2-site Cr occupancy of $0.0008(6)$. X-ray MEM analysis shows no detectable Cr-like density at T2. Magnetic susceptibility indicates weak antiferromagnetic interactions with $θ_{\mathrm{W}} = -4.78(7)$ K and no ordering above 1.8 K.
summary: This paper demonstrates that Cr3+ ions in a milarite-type structure occupy octahedral sites with high selectivity, preventing significant antisite exchange. It reveals that while the structure forms a honeycomb lattice, the magnetic interactions are weak because the intervening Li+ ions act as electronic insulators in the exchange path.
main_problem: The study investigates whether Cr3+ ions selectively occupy the octahedral A-site or undergo antisite exchange with the tetrahedral T2 site in a milarite-type framework, and examines the resulting magnetic connectivity.
main_result: The researchers proved that Cr3+ is selectively accommodated in the octahedral A-site with negligible T2 occupancy, and found that the magnetic connectivity is weak due to the inert Li+ ions in the exchange pathway.
method: The study uses solid-state synthesis, Rietveld refinement of powder X-ray diffraction data, Maximum Entropy Method (MEM) analysis, and magnetic susceptibility measurements.
model_or_system: The system is KNa2Cr2Li3Si12O30, a chromium analogue of the mineral sugilite, featuring a milarite-type framework with a honeycomb-like Cr sublattice and an extended Cr-O-Li-O-Cr exchange path.
key_observables: Antisite parameter (x), lattice parameters, magnetic susceptibility, Curie-Weiss temperature (theta_W), and effective magnetic moment (mu_eff).
important_parameters: theta_W = -4.78(7) K, mu_eff = 3.763(6) uB, and Cr-O distances of approximately 1.948 A.
main_assumptions: The structural analysis assumes a composition-conserving antisite model for Cr/Li exchange.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the projected honeycomb arrangement of the Cr sublattice and the Cr-O-Li-O-Cr magnetic coupling pathway; Figure 4 displays temperature-dependent magnetic susceptibility and its Curie-Weiss fit.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the Cr-analogue of sugilite, details the synthesis and structural characterization (XRD, MEM), presents magnetic susceptibility data, and concludes with design rules for creating honeycomb and kagome magnets.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Fig. 1. Crystal structure of KNa2Cr2Li3Si12O30. (a) Side view highlighting the layered framework separated by the [Si12O30] double-ring unit. (b) Projection along the c axis, showing the honeycomb-like arrangement of the A-site sublattice and the tetrahedral T2 sites linking neighboring A sites. (c) Schematic illustration of a representative extended Cr-O-Li-O-Cr-type exchange path.

Fig. 2. Powder X-ray diffraction pattern and Rietveld refinement profile of KNa2Cr2Li3Si12O30. The observed, calculated, and difference patterns are shown together with the Bragg positions of the main phase and the minor α-cristobalite impurity.

Fig. 3. Maximum entropy analysis (MEM) of KNa2Cr2Li3Si12O30. (a) Structural projection indicating the C, A, and T2 sites. (b) Corresponding MEM electron-density map. (c) Line profile of the MEM electron density along the C-A-T2-A-C path, showing strong density maxima near A and no comparable central peak at T2.
Fig. 4. Temperature dependence of the magnetic susceptibility and inverse susceptibility of KNa2Cr2Li3Si12O30 measured at 0.1 T. The broken line indicates the Curie-Weiss fit over 100-300 K.
